groups: Hepatitis A: Recommended for all travelers, as it is easily contracted through food or water, even at high-end resorts.
Typhoid: Recommended if you are traveling outside major tourist areas, staying for an extended time, or eating street food.
MMR: Protects against measles, mumps, and rubella. (The CDC recommends international travelers be fully vaccinated).
Tdap/Td: Tetanus, diphtheria, and pertussis booster.
Influenza: An annual flu shot is recommended depending on the season.
Polio: An adult booster is recommended by many clinics for global travel.
Hepatitis B: Recommended for long stays, individuals anticipating intimate contact, or those who may receive medical/dental care in Mexico.
Rabies: Recommended for animal handlers, veterinarians, cyclists, or remote explorers at higher risk of animal bites.
Dengue: A dengue vaccine is available and recommended for those visiting high-risk urban and rural areas (especially below 2,300 meters).
Cholera (Dukoral): Some clinics strongly advise this oral vaccine, which also provides cross-protection against common strains of traveler's diarrhea.
Malaria: Only required for travel to specific, highly rural regions in states like Campeche, Chiapas, and southern Chihuahua.
